global_notes: With 56 beats over 147.8s (~2.6s average) and 49 of 56 cuts added beyond the pipeline's editorial group, this is a heavily re-cut piece where the editor is driving rhythm rather than deferring to the source logic. The near-even front/overhead split (29/27) suggests a classic alternation pattern — overhead for prep actions where hand choreography and ingredient placement read best (chopping, seasoning, assembly), front for hero moments and transformations (the lobster going in, the bake reveal, final plating) where dimensionality and steam/sheen sell the dish. Pacing likely tightens through the prep-to-technique stretch as beats stack, then opens up on the transformation beat for a held phrase before a punchier plating sequence. The high "added" count relative to the pipeline's 2 editorial picks indicates the editor is treating the music as primary structure — cutting on beats through dense instructional passages and probably using flash cuts or micro-montages to compress repetitive prep. Expect speed ramps or a brief stop-motion flourish around ingredient assembly to bridge the instructional density, with the front camera reserved as the "payoff" angle after overhead-heavy prep runs.
